title: Government policies during the Civil War
description: Test your skills on how Lincoln's leadership during the Civil War!
topic_path: Government policies during the Civil War | 5.9
Government policies during the Civil War

assessment_items.question_content:
> “We are to meet in the field men who fight for what they consider their rights, and for their homes; and I believe they will contend desperately and frantically. They fight on their own soil, and under more favorable auspices than our Revolutionary fathers; and the issue is as doubtful as it is to be sanguinary. We are certain, however, of being on the right side: constitutional liberty cannot survive the loss of unity in the government.” 

>-Source: Captain Henry Sanford Gansevoort, in a letter to his father, [Memorial of Henry Sanford Gansevoort, Captain Fifth Artillery ... Colonel Thirteenth New York State Volunteer Cavalry . . .](https://books.google.com/books?id=pbMEAAAAYAAJ) 1861

**The excerpt reflects many Americans’ belief at the time that the United States fought in the Civil War to:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: preserve the Union.
abolish the practice of slavery.
protect from foreign invasion.
extend the United States’ borders.

assessment_items.question_content:
> “We are to meet in the field men who fight for what they consider their rights, and for their homes; and I believe they will contend desperately and frantically. They fight on their own soil, and under more favorable auspices than our Revolutionary fathers; and the issue is as doubtful as it is to be sanguinary. We are certain, however, of being on the right side: constitutional liberty cannot survive the loss of unity in the government.” 

>-Source: Captain Henry Sanford Gansevoort, in a letter to his father, [Memorial of Henry Sanford Gansevoort, Captain Fifth Artillery ... Colonel Thirteenth New York State Volunteer Cavalry . . .](https://books.google.com/books?id=pbMEAAAAYAAJ) 1861

**A historian would most likely use this passage to illustrate which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: why supporters of the Union fought in the Civil War
why southerners seceded from the Union
why Lincoln issued the Emancipation Proclamation
why the Confederate states surrendered to the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
> “The members have met their constituents and learned of marvellous conversions to the wisdom of emancipation . . . That to proclaim emancipation would secure the sympathy of Europe and the whole civilized world, which now saw no other reason for the strife than national pride and ambition, an unwillingness to abridge our domain and power. No other step would be so potent to prevent foreign intervention.” 

>-Source: President Abraham Lincoln, Reply to Emancipation Memorial Presented by Chicago Christians of All Denominations, 1862

**The key concern that the excerpt was designed to address was the:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the threat of the Confederacy gaining foreign allies.
the creation of an international peacekeeping force.
the end of the US naval blockade in the South.
the rise in immigration to the South during the war.

assessment_items.question_content:
> “The members have met their constituents and learned of marvellous conversions to the wisdom of emancipation . . . That to proclaim emancipation would secure the sympathy of Europe and the whole civilized world, which now saw no other reason for the strife than national pride and ambition, an unwillingness to abridge our domain and power. No other step would be so potent to prevent foreign intervention.” 

>-Source: President Abraham Lincoln, Reply to Emancipation Memorial Presented by Chicago Christians of All Denominations, 1862

**According to the passage, Lincoln thought the Emancipation Proclamation was necessary for which of the following reasons?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to keep foreign countries from supporting the Confederate government
to recruit formerly enslaved people to serve in the US army as soldiers
to prevent the Confederacy from using enslaved people in their military
to abolish slavery across the United States and uphold the Founders’ values

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/704340d70507a9670f65a56882075781.jpg)

>-Source: N. Flayderman & Co. (via Cowan's Auctions), [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Come_and_Join_Us_Brothers,_by_the_Supervisory_Committee_For_Recruiting_Colored_Regiments.jpg) 1865


**The image most directly reflects which of the following developments during the Civil War?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the enlistment of black people after they escaped enslavement in the South
the recruitment of enslaved people by the Confederate Army in exchange for their freedom
the use of Hessian regiments by the Confederacy to deal with the US Army’s overwhelming forces
the extension of citizenship to any man in the North who fought for the US army

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/704340d70507a9670f65a56882075781.jpg)

>-Source: N. Flayderman & Co. (via Cowan's Auctions), [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Come_and_Join_Us_Brothers,_by_the_Supervisory_Committee_For_Recruiting_Colored_Regiments.jpg) 1865


**Which of the following changes to the United States during the Civil War most directly contributed to the development depicted in the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the order from President Lincoln that freed enslaved people in the South
the introduction of a law that required military service from free black people
the creation of an  Underground Railroad that relocated formerly enslaved people
the decision by the Confederate states to arm the people they enslaved

assessment_items.question_content:
> “My paramount object in this struggle is to save the Union, and is not either to save or destroy slavery. If I could save the Union without freeing any slave I would do it; and if I could save it by freeing all the slaves I would; and if I could save it by freeing some and leaving others alone I would also do that.”

>-Source: Abraham Lincoln, to Horace Greeley, *New York Times,* 1862


**The excerpt most strongly suggests that in 1862 which of the following was correct?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Lincoln’s primary goal for the Civil War was to reunify the country.
Lincoln aimed to end slavery as it would help keep the country together.
Lincoln only intended to end slavery in the Confederate states.
Lincoln’s intention in the Civil War was to destroy the Southern states.

assessment_items.question_content:
> “My paramount object in this struggle is to save the Union, and is not either to save or destroy slavery. If I could save the Union without freeing any slave I would do it; and if I could save it by freeing all the slaves I would; and if I could save it by freeing some and leaving others alone I would also do that.”

>-Source: Abraham Lincoln, to Horace Greeley, *New York Times,* 1862


**The excerpt most directly reflects the US government’s efforts to do which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: unite the North and the South under the federal government
abolish the institution of slavery to avoid future similar conflicts
stop the extension of slavery to the western territories
extend citizenship to all persons born in the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
> “There have been men base enough to propose to me to return to slavery the Black warriors of Port Hudson and Olustee, and thus win the respect of the masters they fought. Should I do so, I should deserve to be damned in time and eternity. Come what will, I will keep my faith with friend and foe. My enemies pretend I am now carrying on this war for the sole purpose of abolition. So long as I am President, it shall be carried on for the sole purpose of restoring the Union.” 

>-Source: President Abraham Lincoln, in an interview with the Honorable John T. Mills, 1864

**The ideas in the excerpt most directly contributed to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the ratification of an amendment ending slavery across the United States
the introduction of a law relocating all black people to Liberia and Ethiopia
the creation of a program that had free black people work in the South for five years
the Supreme Court ruling in *Dred Scott v. Sandford,* which stated that black people were not citizens

assessment_items.question_content:
> “There have been men base enough to propose to me to return to slavery the Black warriors of Port Hudson and Olustee, and thus win the respect of the masters they fought. Should I do so, I should deserve to be damned in time and eternity. Come what will, I will keep my faith with friend and foe. My enemies pretend I am now carrying on this war for the sole purpose of abolition. So long as I am President, it shall be carried on for the sole purpose of restoring the Union.” 

>-Source: President Abraham Lincoln, in an interview with the Honorable John T. Mills, 1864

**Which of the following most likely helped to prompt Lincoln’s message in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the creation of military regiments of free black soldiers
the order of the Emancipation Proclamation in the South
the promise of British support if the United States abolished slavery
the surrender of President Jefferson Davis to the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0879a0fd4948bb6f9b9c415bf0ed0928.jpg)

>-Source: Thomas Nast, “Doctor Lincoln’s New Elixir of Life — for the Southern States,” [Library of Congress,](http://www.loc.gov/pictures/item/cph29479/) 1862

**The ideas expressed through the image reveal that in 1862, which of the following was most true of the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the United States’s war aims came to include ending slavery
the United States’ only goal was to reunite the country
the Union planned to extend slavery
the Union was ending slavery in the border states

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0879a0fd4948bb6f9b9c415bf0ed0928.jpg)

>-Source: Thomas Nast, “Doctor Lincoln’s New Elixir of Life — for the Southern States,” [Library of Congress,](http://www.loc.gov/pictures/item/cph29479/) 1862

**The image was created most directly in response to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the order of the Emancipation Proclamation
the ratification of the Thirteenth Amendment
the introduction of the Homestead Act
the surrender of the Confederacy to the United States
